extraction.one_line_summary: ACKERMANN Pascal won Stage 2 by sharply cutting right through a gap that opened when VAN POPPEL Danny hesitated, with HODEG Álvaro José following through for second.
tactical_patterns: HODEG Álvaro José surrendered MØRKØV Michael's wheel mid-leadout at 12th position, destroying Deceuninck sprint train structure before finale.
VAN POPPEL Danny held back creating gap instead of closing when ACKERMANN and HODEG accelerated, tactical hesitation cost positioning.
Polish national team riders sandwiched MØRKØV Michael disrupting his leadout timing, forcing premature sprint from suboptimal position.
Lotto-Jumbo sprint train collapsed allowing BOASSON HAGEN Edvald to freelance, revealing team leadout discipline breakdown under pressure.

rider_signals.ACKERMANN Pascal: positive: read VAN POPPEL hesitation instantly and executed aggressive right-side cutoff at high speed to steal stage
rider_signals.HODEG Álvaro José: negative: abandoned team leadout wheel mid-sequence dropping to 12th, then followed ACKERMANN rather than initiating own sprint
rider_signals.VAN POPPEL Danny: negative: hesitation when gap opened cost sprint positioning despite being on correct wheel
rider_signals.MØRKØV Michael: neutral: isolated by Polish riders disrupting leadout but maintained effort despite tactical breakdown
rider_signals.BOASSON HAGEN Edvald: neutral: freelanced away from disintegrating Lotto-Jumbo train attempting independent sprint
